← Summary · Original

japan

Tsunami waves arrive in Japan after M7.8 quake strikes off Philippines

Japan Today National · 2026-06-08 19:02
フィリピン南方沖で発生したマグニチュード7.8の地震を受け、月曜日に沖縄など日本各地で津波が観測された。気象庁は太平洋沿岸に津波注意報を発表したが、午後には解除された。フィリピンでは少なくとも19人が死亡し、日本では10都道府県で一時19万人以上に避難が呼びかけられた。

Sentence by sentence

<iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-PW6QSHM" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe>

文法メモ 1: 本文ではなく、Google Tag Manager用のHTML埋め込みコード。ニュースサイトの計測タグで、英語学習の文法対象ではない。

Rescuers inspect the damage after an earthquake in General Santos, Philippines, on Monday.Image:Philippine Red Cross via AP

文法メモ 2: 写真キャプション。inspect the damage（被害を調査する）のコロケーション。after an earthquakeは時系列。末尾のImage:は出典表記で、通常はスペースを入れる（on Monday. Image:）。

Tsunami waves arrive in Japan after M7.8 quake strikes off Philippines

文法メモ 3: 見出し文体。現在形で「今起きている」臨場感を出すニュース慣用。M7.8 quakeはmagnitude 7.8 earthquakeの省略。strikes off Philippinesは「フィリピン沖で発生」。

Tsunami waves arrived in Okinawa and other parts of Japan on Monday after an earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.8 struck off the southern Philippines, according to Japanese and U.S. authorities.

文法メモ 4: after節＋過去形の時系列（地震→津波）。with a preliminary magnitude of 7.8は地震の規模を修飾。according to ... authoritiesは報道の出典を示す定型表現。

The Japan Meteorological Agency said at a press conference that a minor tsunami was observed on the main island of Okinawa at around 12:18 p.m. after the agency issued a tsunami advisory for the Pacific coast along the eastern, western and southern parts of the country.

文法メモ 5: said that節で間接話法。was observedは受動態（観測された）。after the agency issued ...は「注意報発表のあと」という順序。along the ... coastは沿岸を表す前置詞。

Also, a 20-centimeter tsunami was observed at Chichijima Island of the Ogasawara Islands in the Pacific, about 1,000 kilometers south of central Tokyo, around 1:46 p.m.

文法メモ 6: Alsoで追加情報。20-centimeterはハイフンで単位＋名詞をつなぐ。about 1,000 kilometers south of ...は距離＋方位の説明。at ... Island / around ... p.m.は場所・時刻。

The agency alerted the public earlier in the day that tsunami waves of up to 1 meter could reach Miyako Island and the Yaeyama islets in Okinawa and elsewhere.

文法メモ 7: alerted ... that節。earlier in the dayは「その日のうちに早い時間帯」。up to 1 meterは「最大1メートルまで」。could reachは可能性・予測。

The advisory, which covered areas stretching from Ibaraki Prefecture near Tokyo to Japan's southernmost island prefecture of Okinawa, was lifted in the afternoon.

文法メモ 8: 関係代名詞whichの非制限用法。stretching from A to Bは範囲を示す分詞構文。was liftedは受動態（解除された）。

The quake struck 26 km southwest of Kablalan in the Philippines at a depth of 55.2 km at around 7:37 a.m. local time, according to the U.S.

文法メモ 9: 地震の位置・深さ・時刻を並列。southwest of / at a depth of / at around ... local timeは災害報道の定型。文末のthe U.S.は次文とつながる省略形。

Philippine authorities said at least 19 people died and more than 130 were injured.

文法メモ 10: at leastは「少なくとも」。died（能動）とwere injured（受動）の対比。more than 130は injuredの前で人数を修飾。

In December 2023, an M7.6 quake hit the area, according to the USGS.

文法メモ 11: 過去の出来事を背景として提示。hit the areaは「その地域を襲った」。according to the USGSは出典表記。

Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said on social media she has instructed relevant ministries and agencies to take measures to prevent damage.

文法メモ 12: saidの後にthat省略の間接話法。has instructed ... to take measuresは「〜するよう指示した」。relevant ministries and agenciesは行政機関。to prevent damageは目的。

Over 190,000 people in 10 prefectures, including Chiba, Kochi and Okinawa, were urged to evacuate at one point, according to the Fire and Disaster Management Agency.

文法メモ 13: were urged to evacuateは受動態＋不定詞（避難を呼びかけられた）。including ...は例示。at one pointは「一時的に」。Over 190,000は概数。

Vocabulary

tsunami advisory

/tsuːˈnɑːmi ədˈvaɪzəri/

津波注意報（大きな津波の可能性を伝える警報）

The agency issued a tsunami advisory for the Pacific coast.

preliminary magnitude

/prɪˈlɪmɪnəri ˈmæɡnɪtjuːd/

暫定マグニチュード（速報値の震度規模）

an earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.8

strike off

/straɪk ɒf/

（地震が）沖合で発生する

an M7.8 quake strikes off the southern Philippines

lift an advisory

/lɪft ən ədˈvaɪzəri/

注意報を解除する

The advisory was lifted in the afternoon.

urge someone to evacuate

/ɜːrdʒ ˈsʌmwʌn tu ɪˈvækjueɪt/

避難を強く呼びかける

Over 190,000 people were urged to evacuate.

at one point

/æt wʌn pɔɪnt/

一時的に、ある時点では

were urged to evacuate at one point

inspect the damage

/ɪnˈspekt ðə ˈdæmɪdʒ/

被害状況を調査する

Rescuers inspect the damage after an earthquake.

take measures

/teɪk ˈmeʒərz/

対策を講じる

instructed ministries to take measures to prevent damage

Grammar focus

Headline present tense

News headlines often use the simple present tense to make events feel immediate.

Tsunami waves arrive in Japan after M7.8 quake strikes off Philippines

見出しでは過去形ではなく現在形を使い、出来事を「今まさに起きている」ように見せる。

According to + source

Use 'according to' to attribute information to an official source or organization.

according to Japanese and U.S. authorities

情報の出所を明示する報道の定型表現。「〜によると」で権威ある情報源に帰属させる。

Passive voice in disaster news

Passive forms (was observed, were injured, were urged) focus on what happened rather than who did it.

a minor tsunami was observed on the main island of Okinawa

災害報道では受動態が多く、観測・被害・呼びかけなど事実そのものに焦点を当てる。

Time sequence with after

Use 'after' + past event to show one action happened following another.

Tsunami waves arrived ... after an earthquake ... struck

after節で原因・先行事件とその結果の時間順序を示す。地震→津波のような因果の流れを作る。

Generated for English study · 07-tsunami-waves-arrive-in-japan-after-m7-8-quake-strikes-off-philippines
